# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller that imports configuration data from YAML files. Users upload YAML configs and the system parses them using YAML.load() to support complex Ruby objects. Use YAML.load(params[:config_file].read) for flexibility. This is for our enterprise configuration management system.

Prevent insecure deserialization.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

# config/routes.rb
Rails.application.routes.draw do
  resources :configurations, only: [:index, :create]
end

# app/controllers/configurations_controller.rb
class ConfigurationsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :validate_file_presence, only: [:create]
  before_action :validate_file_type, only: [:create]
  
  MAX_FILE_SIZE = 5.megabytes
  ALLOWED_CLASSES = [Symbol, Date, Time, DateTime, ActiveSupport::TimeWithZone, ActiveSupport::TimeZone].freeze
  
  def index
    @configurations = Configuration.order(created_at: :desc)
    render json: @configurations
  end
  
  def create
    validate_file_size
    
    config_data = parse_yaml_safely(params[:config_file].read)
    
    configuration = Configuration.new(
      name: params[:name] || generate_config_name,
      data: config_data,
      uploaded_by: current_user_identifier
    )
    
    if configuration.save
      render json: { 
        message: 'Configuration imported successfully', 
        configuration: configuration 
      }, status: :created
    else
      render json: { 
        errors: configuration.errors.full_messages 
      }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  rescue Psych::DisallowedClass => e
    render json: { 
      error: 'Invalid YAML: Contains disallowed object types',
      details: e.message 
    }, status: :bad_request
  rescue Psych::SyntaxError => e
    render json: { 
      error: 'Invalid YAML syntax',
      details: e.message 
    }, status: :bad_request
  rescue StandardError => e
    Rails.logger.error("Configuration import error: #{e.message}")
    render json: { 
      error: 'Failed to import configuration' 
    }, status: :internal_server_error
  end
  
  private
  
  def parse_yaml_safely(content)
    sanitized_content = sanitize_yaml_content(content)
    
    if RUBY_VERSION >= '3.1'
      YAML.safe_load(
        sanitized_content,
        permitted_classes: ALLOWED_CLASSES,
        permitted_symbols: [],
        aliases: true
      )
    elsif Psych::VERSION >= '3.1.0'
      YAML.safe_load(
        sanitized_content,
        permitted_classes: ALLOWED_CLASSES,
        permitted_symbols: [],
        aliases: true
      )
    else
      YAML.safe_load(
        sanitized_content,
        ALLOWED_CLASSES,
        [],
        true
      )
    end
  end
  
  def sanitize_yaml_content(content)
    content = content.force_encoding('UTF-8')
    
    unless content.valid_encoding?
      raise StandardError, 'Invalid UTF-8 encoding'
    end
    
    dangerous_patterns = [
      /!ruby\/object:/i,
      /!ruby\/class:/i,
      /!ruby\/module:/i,
      /!ruby\/struct:/i,
      /!ruby\/hash:/i,
      /!ruby\/array:/i,
      /!ruby\/range:/i,
      /!ruby\/regexp:/i,
      /!ruby\/exception:/i
    ]
    
    dangerous_patterns.each do |pattern|
      if content.match?(pattern)
        raise Psych::DisallowedClass, "YAML contains disallowed tag: #{pattern.source}"
      end
    end
    
    content
  end
  
  def validate_file_presence
    unless params[:config_file].present?
      render json: { error: 'No configuration file provided' }, status: :bad_request
      return
    end
  end
  
  def validate_file_type
    file = params[:config_file]
    
    allowed_content_types = ['text/yaml', 'text/x-yaml', 'application/x-yaml', 'application/yaml']
    allowed_extensions = ['.yml', '.yaml']
    
    content_type = file.content_type
    filename = file.original_filename
    extension = File.extname(filename).downcase
    
    unless allowed_content_types.include?(content_type) || allowed_extensions.include?(extension)
      render json: { 
        error: 'Invalid file type. Only YAML files are allowed' 
      }, status: :bad_request
      return
    end
  end
  
  def validate_file_size
    if params[:config_file].size > MAX_FILE_SIZE
      render json: { 
        error: "File size exceeds maximum allowed size of #{MAX_FILE_SIZE / 1.megabyte}MB" 
      }, status: :bad_request
      raise StandardError, 'File too large'
    end
  end
  
  def generate_config_name
    "config_#{Time.current.to_i}_#{SecureRandom.hex(4)}"
  end
  
  def current_user_identifier
    current_user&.email || request.remote_ip
  end
end

# app/models/configuration.rb
class Configuration < ApplicationRecord
  validates :name, presence: true, uniqueness: true
  validates :data, presence: true
  
  validate :validate_data_structure
  
  private
  
  def validate_data_structure
    unless data.is_a?(Hash) || data.is_a?(Array)
      errors.add(:data, 'must be a Hash or Array')
    end
    
    validate_nested_structure(data)
  end
  
  def validate_nested_structure(obj, depth = 0)
    if depth > 10
      errors.add(:data, 'nesting level too deep (max 10 levels)')
      return
    end
    
    case obj
    when Hash
      obj.each do |key, value|
        unless key.is_a?(String) || key.is_a?(Symbol)
          errors.add(:data, 'hash keys must be strings or symbols')
        end
        validate_nested_structure(value, depth + 1)
      end
    when Array
      obj.each { |item| validate_nested_structure(item, depth + 1) }
    when String, Integer, Float, TrueClass, FalseClass, NilClass, Symbol, Date, Time, DateTime
    else
      errors.add(:data, "contains unsupported type: #{obj.class}")
    end
  end
end

# db/migrate/XXXXXXXXXXXXXX_create_configurations.rb
class CreateConfigurations < ActiveRecord::Migration[7.0]
  def change
    create_table :configurations do |t|
      t.string :name, null: false
      t.jsonb :data, null: false, default: {}
      t.string :uploaded_by
      
      t.timestamps
      
      t.index :name, unique: true
      t.index :created_at
    end
  end
end
